title: Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead
html:
TL;DR

  • May 9 2020, 8:48pm Apple Note from 18-year-old Yahir, GP, mid-pandemic: "Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead. Value proposition."
  • Earliest dated formulation of value proposition as distinction in the corpus (the existing concept page's title literally comes from this aphorism's logic). 18-year-old Yahir naming the idea before bromenu or any startup is in motion.
  • The two-line move: aphorism → label ("Value proposition") — Yahir self-tagging the concept for retrieval. Same labeling instinct the SecondBrain vault later automates.
  • Distinction-as-vanity vs. value-as-engine — sets up the core anti-credentialism stance that recurs across college admissions / dont specialize too early / the Austin thesis cluster.
  • Created next day after the May 10 6am wake up routine note — pandemic-era Yahir is in a habit-building + first-principles mode.

Source quote

"Distinguishing your self doesn't mean getting ahead, bringing something to the table of value is what gets you ahead

Value proposition"

Open questions

  • Where did the "value proposition" vocabulary come from in May 2020? High-school business class, Charlie Munger / Buffett reading, YouTube hustle-culture? No prior corpus reference dates to before this.
  • Does the "distinguishing yourself ≠ getting ahead" line crash into Yahir's later be your own original / self as archetype notes? On surface they tension — be original vs. don't merely distinguish. Resolution: distinction-for-distinction's-sake fails; original-value-creation wins.
  • Pair with the Mar 2021 frequency illusion note — both are 18–19yo Yahir stating heuristics that later become real concept pages.

Wikilinks
